description_long: A cluster of five stylized mushrooms is depicted, each with a thick, cylindrical brown stem and a wide, flat cap. The caps are a vibrant red-orange color adorned with large, irregular white spots, reminiscent of the classic Amanita muscaria. The mushrooms vary in size, with some caps being more domed and others flatter, and the stems are slightly tapered at the base. The surface of the caps appears matte and slightly rough, with the white spots distributed unevenly. No gills are visible beneath the caps, and the stems are solid and robust. The group is presented as if growing together, making it suitable for placement in parks, gardens, or decorative urban green spaces.
description: A group of five red-orange mushrooms with white spots and thick brown stems, featuring flat to domed caps and a matte, slightly rough surface.
description_view_0: Three mushrooms are most prominent, showing their red-orange caps with white spots and thick brown stems, with two mushrooms partially obscured in the background.
description_view_1: The mushrooms are seen more from the side, highlighting the varying heights and the flatness of some caps, with all five stems visible.
description_view_2: A top-down angle reveals the full spread of the caps, emphasizing the irregular white spots and the relative sizes of each mushroom.
description_view_3: A frontal view displays all five mushrooms clearly, with the largest cap in the center and the others arranged around it, showing the group structure.
